voice-clone.label: Clone Voice
voice-clone.description: Generate multi-speaker dialogue with emotion and nonverbal sounds

prompt.label: Dialogue script
prompt.description: Multi-speaker script. Tag turns with [S1] / [S2] and add nonverbal cues like (laughs) or (sighs).

voice_ref.label: Voice reference (Dia)
voice_ref.description: Optional 5–10 s clip whose voice the output imitates (in-context voice cloning). The transcript field auto-fills on upload — edit it if the auto-transcription needs fixing. Leave empty for a random voice.

seed.label: Dia voice seed
seed.description: Random seed; -1 for a new voice each run

cfg_scale.label: Dia guidance scale
cfg_scale.description: How strongly generation follows the script

temperature.label: Dia temperature
temperature.description: Sampling temperature — higher is more varied

max_tokens.label: Dia max new tokens
max_tokens.description: Upper bound on generated audio length (~86 tokens ≈ 1 s)

description:
Dia is a 1.6B-parameter text-to-dialogue model from Nari Labs that
generates ultra-realistic multi-speaker conversation in a single pass.
Mark turns with [S1] / [S2] speaker tags and sprinkle nonverbal cues
like (laughs) or (sighs); optionally supply a short reference clip to
clone a voice. GPU-only.
notes: GPU only. Upstream CPU inference is "to be added soon" (not yet supported), so no Dockerfile.cpu / CPU image ships. inference.py still detects the device via torch.cuda.is_available() for graceful local runs.
Two-speaker dialogue via [S1]/[S2] tags plus a fixed set of nonverbal cues ((laughs), (sighs), (coughs), …). Voice is random per run unless seed is fixed or a reference clip is supplied.
voice_ref enables Dia's in-context voice cloning. The exact processor keyword for the audio prompt should be confirmed against the pinned transformers version at first build.
Weights (~6 GB) for nari-labs/Dia-1.6B-0626 are baked at build via snapshot_download (weights-first layer). The Descript Audio Codec (DAC) used for decoding is pulled when the model is verify-loaded.

torch / torchaudio / transformers ship UNPINNED in the Dockerfile as a starting point; pin exact versions from pip freeze after the first clean build (Container Build Rule). transformers must be a version that includes DiaForConditionalGeneration.
Output is 44.1 kHz mono (Dia/DAC native rate) — verify at first build.

strategies.label: Fix grammar
strategies.instruction: Fix spelling and grammar mistakes only. Do not change the meaning, structure, or intent of the text. Return only the corrected text.

strategies.label: Optimise for Dia
strategies.instruction:
Reformat the text into Dia dialogue script syntax. Your only job is to add, correct, and normalise speaker tags. Never add, remove, rewrite, paraphrase, shorten, or expand the words the user wants spoken — preserve them exactly, in their original order, with their original punctuation.
Tag rules: - Always begin the script with [S1]. - Alternate [S1] and [S2] for each new speaker turn. Dia requires
  strict alternation — never emit the same tag twice in a row.
- If two consecutive turns belong to the same speaker, merge
  their spoken content under one tag (joining the words in order).
- If the input starts with [S2] or otherwise breaks alternation,
  re-label the tags so the script starts with [S1] and alternates
  cleanly. Keep the spoken content in its original order.
- If the input is one continuous passage with no speaker
  indicators, treat it as a single-speaker monologue: wrap the
  entire text in one [S1] tag, unchanged.

Formatting vs. content: - Speaker attributions ("Alice said:", "Narrator:", "Bob asked")
  are formatting — convert them into [S1]/[S2] tags, do not keep
  the prose.
- Stage directions, scene descriptions, and bracketed actions
  are formatting — drop them.
- Dia's recognised nonverbal cues are content if the user wrote
  them — keep them in place: (laughs), (chuckle), (sighs),
  (gasps), (coughs), (clears throat), (sniffs), (groans),
  (whistles), (sings), (mumbles). Drop any cue not on this list
  (Dia would speak it literally otherwise).
- All other spoken words are content — preserve them verbatim.
Do not invent new cues, character descriptions, voice notes, emotion labels, or extra dialogue. Return only the reformatted script — no preamble, explanation, or trailing commentary.
